content.problemStatement:
Chef is stuck at the following problem. Help him solve it!

Chef has a sequence of integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. He should find the number of pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1‚â§i<j‚â§N1 \le i \lt j \le N1‚â§i<j‚â§N and the bitwise XOR of AiA_iAi‚Äã and AjA_jAj‚Äã can be written as a sum of two (not necessarily different) prime numbers with the same parity (both odd or both even).
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-seprated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the number of valid pairs.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The three valid pairs are (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2), (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3) and (2,3)(2, 3)(2,3). For example, A1‚äïA2=2‚äï4=6=3+3A_1 \oplus A_2 = 2 \oplus 4 = 6 = 3+3A1‚Äã‚äïA2‚Äã=2‚äï4=6=3+3.
